title: lastrea hallieri
intro: Lastrea hallieri, commonly known as the Hall's fern or Hall's lastrea, is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. It is a medicinal plant known for its various health benefits, including its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant. The therapeutic actions of Lastrea hallieri include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Lastrea hallieri include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Lastrea hallieri can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing skin irritation, allergic reactions, and respiratory issues. Ingesting parts of the plant can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to the presence of toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap may cause eye irritation, redness, and temporary blindness. Improper use of Lastrea hallieri can also trigger asthma attacks or exacerbate existing respiratory conditions in sensitive individuals.
